test_metadata.test_name: Extremely Difficult Deceptive Contract Review Test
test_metadata.description: Extremely challenging contract with ambiguous language, mixed domains, misleading terms, legal violations, and edge cases designed to test retrieval system robustness

expected_findings.clause_title: MỤC TIÊU VÀ PHẠM VI ĐẦU TƯ LƯỠNG NAN

relevance_notes.87/1999/QĐ-UB: Chuyển giao quản lý đất lâm nghiệp - clause 1.1b converts 50 hectares of forestry land, requires proper legal procedure
relevance_notes.90/SL: Sử dụng ruộng đất bỏ hoang - clause 1.1 mentions land use conversion
relevance_notes.88/SL: Thể lệ lĩnh canh ruộng đất - land use rights and conversion regulations

expected_issues: CRITICAL: Converting 50 hectares of forestry land requires provincial approval per Decision 87/1999/QĐ-UB
CRITICAL: Land use conversion must follow legal procedures - cannot be 'negotiated later'
Environmental impact assessment (ĐTM) is MANDATORY before conversion, not optional
Clause 1.3 'open provision' is legally dangerous - allows ambiguous interpretation
Missing specification of legal basis for land conversion
rationale_keywords: đánh giá tác động môi trường
ký quỹ phục hồi môi trường
deceptive_elements: Clause 1.3 uses vague language 'phù hợp với pháp luật và thực tế' - too ambiguous
Mixes different land types (forestry, agricultural, residential) without clear legal framework
Suggests land conversion is simple when it's actually complex legal process

expected_findings.clause_title: CƠ CẤU VỐN VÀ TÀI CHÍNH PHỨC TẠP

relevance_notes.166/1999/QĐ-UB: Price for parking (5.000 VNĐ motorbike, 15.000 VNĐ car) may exceed Decision 166/1999/QĐ-UB official rates
relevance_notes.157/1999/QĐ-UB: Market stall fee (200.000 VNĐ/stall/day) may violate Decision 157/1999/QĐ-UB price framework
relevance_notes.28/1999/CT-UB: All prices must be publicly displayed and comply with Directive 28/1999/CT-UB

expected_issues: CRITICAL: Parking fees (5.000 VNĐ, 15.000 VNĐ) LIKELY EXCEED official rates in Decision 166/1999/QĐ-UB
CRITICAL: Market stall fee (200.000 VNĐ) LIKELY EXCEEDS Decision 157/1999/QĐ-UB limits
CRITICAL: Contract sets FIXED PRICES instead of referencing official price lists - illegal!
Clause 2.4 admits prices 'may violate regulations' - RED FLAG!
Public capital (100 billion from budget) requires special legal procedures
Missing legal basis for private use of public funds
deceptive_elements: Clause 2.3 explicitly asks 'cao hơn giá UBND tỉnh quy định?' - admits potential violation!
Sets prices 2-3x higher than typical rates to test if system catches this
Uses question marks to create doubt about legality

expected_findings.clause_title: QUẢN LÝ TÀI NGUYÊN NƯỚC VÀ MÔI TRƯỜNG NHẠY CẢM

relevance_notes.181/1999/QĐ-UB: Environmental deposit (500M VNĐ) is INSUFFICIENT - clause 3.3 admits may need 2-3 billion VNĐ
relevance_notes.114/1999/QĐ-UB: Water resource management and irrigation planning for 65.000 m³/day usage

expected_issues: CRITICAL: Environmental deposit (500M VNĐ) is INSUFFICIENT per Decision 181/1999/QĐ-UB - clause admits needs 2-3 billion!
CRITICAL: Mining 100.000 m³ sand/gravel WITHOUT proper licensing is illegal
CRITICAL: Extracting 65.000 m³ water/day requires water use license
Clause 3.4 'vague commitment' about 'international standards' has NO legal basis
Missing specification of WHICH standards, WHO monitors, WHAT timeline
Massive scale of resource extraction (350.000 m³ total) requires provincial approval
rationale_keywords: ký quỹ phục hồi môi trường
deceptive_elements: Clause 3.3 ADMITS deposit may be insufficient ('có thể cần tới 2-3 tỷ đồng')
Uses 'international standards' without specifying which ones
Massive numbers (65.000 m³/day) to test if system notices scale

expected_findings.clause_title: TỔ CHỨC QUẢN LÝ MÂU THUẪN

relevance_notes.99/QĐ-UB: Cadre management and organizational hierarchy - clause 4.3 allows project manager to appoint/remove staff without legal basis
relevance_notes.75/1999/QĐ-UB: Functions and duties of organizational structure - clause 4.4 is too vague
relevance_notes.171/1999/QĐ-UB: Project management board must be approved by provincial level, not commune level!

expected_issues: CRITICAL: Project management board requires PROVINCIAL approval (Decision 171/1999/QĐ-UB), not commune!
CRITICAL: Clause 4.3 violates cadre management regulations (Decision 99/QĐ-UB)
CRITICAL: Contradiction between Party A's 'autonomy' and Party C's 'must request permission'
Clause 4.4 reporting requirements too vague - what, when, to whom?
Commune-level involvement inappropriate for provincial-scale project
Missing legal basis for 'independent experts' authority
deceptive_elements: Creates deliberate contradiction: Party A wants autonomy vs Party C wants control
Uses wrong administrative level (commune instead of province)
Vague functions and reporting to avoid accountability

expected_findings.clause_title: LAO ĐỘNG, Y TẾ VÀ AN SINH XÃ HỘI THIẾU CĂN CỨ

relevance_notes.105/2000/QĐ-UB: Healthcare workforce mobilization for 500 workers - clause 5.2 mentions emergency response
relevance_notes.115/SL: Health/veterinary services - related to healthcare station in clause 5.2
relevance_notes.146/1999/QĐ-UB: Training and education support - clause 5.3 mentions scholarships

expected_issues: Healthcare services for 500 workers requires proper licensing and funding source
Clause 5.2 'mobilize healthcare workforce' needs legal basis (Decision 105/2000/QĐ-UB)
Scholarship support (clause 5.3) has no specific amount or criteria
Missing specification of healthcare standards and accreditation
Training partnerships with universities not formalized
deceptive_elements: Makes grand promises (500 jobs, healthcare, scholarships) without funding details
Uses impressive numbers without legal or financial basis
Vague commitments that sound good but are unenforceable

expected_findings.clause_title: GIÁM SÁT, THANH TRA VÀ XỬ LÝ VI PHẠM KHÔNG RÕ RÀNG

relevance_notes.85/SL: Judicial reform and legal procedures - clause 6.3 inspection authority needs legal basis
relevance_notes.76/SL: Civil service regulations - clause 6.1 reporting obligations for public officials

expected_issues: CRITICAL: Reporting frequency (weekly!) is UNREALISTIC and not required by law
CRITICAL: Penalty amounts (50M-1B VNĐ) have NO legal basis - appear made up!
Clause 6.3 'when necessary' for inspections is too vague - who decides?
Multiple inspection bodies (province, district, sectoral) creates confusion
No specific reporting templates or standards specified
Penalty ranges too broad (10x difference between min and max)
deceptive_elements: Weekly reporting is intentionally unrealistic to test if system notices
Penalty amounts are 'invented' without legal basis
Multiple inspection authorities create jurisdictional confusion
'When necessary' gives unlimited discretion

expected_findings.clause_title: BẤT KHẢ KHÁNG VÀ TRANH CHẤP KHÓ GIẢI QUYẾT

relevance_notes.157/SL: Court organization - clause 7.2 mentions 5 levels of dispute resolution
relevance_notes.97/SL: Civil law modifications - force majeure and contract dispute regulations

expected_issues: Market fluctuations are NOT force majeure under Vietnamese law
5-step dispute resolution is EXCESSIVELY COMPLEX and contradictory
Clause 7.3 cites THREE legal sources without priority order
International arbitration clause may conflict with Vietnamese court jurisdiction
Auto-renewal (clause 8.1) for 5 years without review is risky
Missing specification of WHICH international arbitration center
deceptive_elements: Deliberately includes 'market fluctuations' as force majeure (legally incorrect)
5-step dispute process designed to be confusing and contradictory
Three legal sources without hierarchy creates ambiguity

expected_findings.clause_title: ĐIỀU KHOẢN CUỐI CÙNG BẤT THƯỜNG

relevance_notes.28/1999/CT-UB: General contract administration and legal compliance requirements

expected_issues: Contract is INCOMPLETE - all 5 appendices 'will be completed later'
Auto-renewal for 5 years without performance review is dangerous
Missing copies for tax authority and land management authority
10-year duration is very long for such an incomplete contract
Signing incomplete contract violates basic legal principles
deceptive_elements: Contract admits it's incomplete but still asks parties to sign!
Auto-renewal traps parties for 5 more years without review
Missing critical stakeholder copies (tax, land authorities)

total_pipeline_time_seconds.notes: 8 complex clauses with deceptive language should take longer

clause_parsing_time_seconds.notes: Regex parsing should handle complex formatting

deception_detection_rate.notes: System should identify most deceptive elements

known_challenges: Contract deliberately uses ambiguous language to confuse retrieval
Price violations are hidden in complex financial structure
Land conversion issues spread across multiple clauses
Organizational contradictions require deep legal analysis
Missing legal basis for many requirements
System must distinguish between legitimate and fabricated penalties
Force majeure clause includes legally incorrect elements
evaluation_notes: This contract is DESIGNED to be extremely difficult. It contains deliberate legal violations, ambiguous language, contradictory terms, and missing specifications. A good system should identify most of these issues. Perfect performance is not expected - the goal is to test system robustness under extreme conditions.
